title: Thayir Sadam (Tempered Curd Rice)

subs.name: cooked millet (thinai/samai) for a lighter version
plant-based yogurt for a dairy-free version

ingredients.name: milk
visual_checks: keeps the rice from turning sour in a lunchbox
ingredients.notes: keeps the rice from turning sour in a lunchbox

subs.name: chana dal
subs.notes: soak 10 minutes first for even frying

steps.text: Pressure cook the rice with 2.5 cups water for 4 whistles so it is softer than usual, or use day-old rice mashed with a splash of hot water.
Let the rice cool until just warm. Mash it lightly with the back of a ladle — you want it creamy, not pasty.
Mix in the curd, milk and salt until smooth. The mixture should be loose; it thickens as it sits.
Heat oil in a small tempering pan. Splutter mustard seeds, add urad dal and fry until golden, then add ginger, green chilli, curry leaves and asafoetida. Fry for 30 seconds.
Pour the tempering over the curd rice and fold through. Garnish with pomegranate or grated carrot. Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled, with pickle.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → plant-based yogurt for a dairy-free version (skip the milk too); rice → cooked millet (thinai/samai) for a lighter version; urad-dal → chana dal (soak 10 minutes first for even frying)
